metadata.description: Search and discover Elegy capabilities on demand. Use this instead of loading all skill definitions into context. Start with skill-search to find relevant capabilities, then use skill-describe to get full invocation details.
metadata.summary: On-demand skill discovery and routing — search, list, and describe Elegy capabilities without loading all definitions into context.
metadata.author: Elegy Contributors

capabilities.name: Search Skills
capabilities.description: Find relevant skills by keyword or task description. Returns compact index-level results by default. Use --detail for capability-level information.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Free-text search query matched against skill names, keywords, triggers, and capability descriptions.

parameters.name: detail
parameters.description: When true, returns capability-level detail including parameters and execution metadata.

output.description: Array of matching skills with match scores, matched capabilities, and match reasons. Includes expandCommand hint for full details.

capabilities.name: Describe Skill
capabilities.description: Get the complete v2 skill definition for a specific skill, including all capabilities with full implementation details, governance, and constraints.

output.description: Complete v2 skill definition JSON with all capabilities, implementation blocks, governance, and constraints.

capabilities.name: List All Skills
capabilities.description: List all available skills with compact index-level metadata. Use --detail for capability-level information.

parameters.name: detail
parameters.description: When true, includes capability-level detail for each skill.

output.description: Array of skills with id, name, description, category, capability count, and lifecycle state.

constraints.description: Discovery responses use progressive disclosure: index (default) is compact, --detail adds capabilities, elegy skills describe returns full definitions. This minimizes context token usage.

triggers.description: When an agent needs to discover which Elegy capabilities are available.
When searching for specific Elegy tools or operations.
When an agent wants to understand all available Elegy capabilities.
When setting up skill routing or progressive context loading.
